ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for chocolate latte cold; mapped to closest parent class 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301710). Chocolate and milk components are not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (coffee-based beverage) used as closest defensible match. No specific code exists for iced chocolate latte in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chocolate latte cold beverage; SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for café beverage variants.

preparation_method: mixed with chocolate syrup or unsweetened cocoa powder

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of cold chocolate latte is approximately 350 g, typical of a medium-sized café serving in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beverages, coffee, mocha, ready-to-drink (SR Legacy ID 171891); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central chocolate milk beverage entries
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'chocolate latte cold' exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for ready-to-drink mocha coffee beverage (FDC ID 171891) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central chocolate milk beverage data. Input per_100g values from the source record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with the USDA adapted profile and have been retained with medium confidence. Monounsaturated fat and polyunsaturated fat are not reported in the primary source for this beverage category and are set to -1. HPB Singapore Foodo was consulted but does not contain a directly matching entry for this beverage.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283; University of Sydney GI Database — chocolate milk analogues (GI 34–43)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'chocolate latte cold' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 43 is estimated from the closest analogous entries: chocolate milk beverages (GI 34–43, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The milk matrix substantially lowers the effective GI of the added sugar component. The upper bound of the chocolate milk GI range (43) is used as a conservative estimate for this beverage, which contains added sugar and espresso in addition to milk and chocolate. This places the beverage in the low GI category (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (43 × 40.0) / 100 = 17.2, rounded to 17.1, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 11.43 × 3.5 = 40.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar (10 g/100 g) in a beverage matrix; caffeine from espresso
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low at 34 mg/100 g, the sugar content of 10 g/100 g in liquid form independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention (AHA 2018); individuals with hypertension should request unsweetened preparation and limit to one serving per day due to caffeine-related acute blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 10 g sugar/100 g; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose spike; dietary fibre only 0.57 g/100 g provides negligible glycaemic modulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: This sugar-sweetened beverage delivers approximately 35 g of sugar per standard 350 g serving, causing a rapid and significant postprandial glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this preparation and request an unsweetened version using unsweetened cocoa powder and no added sugar, or substitute with a sugar-free syrup.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 2.0 g/100 g from milk; trans fat at 0.09 g/100 g; high added sugar (10 g/100 g) contributing to dyslipidaemia risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (2.0 g/100 g), trace trans fat (0.09 g/100 g), and high added sugar (10 g/100 g) in a daily beverage may contribute to adverse lipid profiles; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption, request unsweetened preparation, and consider low-fat or plant-based milk alternatives to reduce saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 10 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; chocolate syrup negates anti-inflammatory benefit of coffee polyphenols
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Added sugar at 10 g/100 g from chocolate syrup promotes inflammatory mediators; an unsweetened version using espresso, unsweetened cocoa powder, and milk without added sugar would be acceptable for rheumatoid arthritis due to the anti-inflammatory chlorogenic acid polyphenols in espresso.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chocolate Latte Cold (冷巧克力拿铁; Latte Coklat Sejuk) is a popular iced café beverage widely available in Singapore and Southeast Asian coffee chains. It is prepared by brewing a shot of espresso, mixing it with chocolate syrup or unsweetened cocoa powder and sugar, combining with fresh whole milk, and pouring the mixture over ice. The result is a creamy, mildly bitter-sweet cold drink with a rich chocolate-coffee flavour and a smooth, slightly frothy texture. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 85.7 kcal, 2.3 g protein, 3.4 g fat (2.0 g saturated), 11.4 g carbohydrate, and 10.0 g sugar, with 34.3 mg sodium and 71.4 mg calcium from the milk component. The high sugar content (10 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without modification. An unsweetened version using unsweetened cocoa powder and no added sugar would significantly reduce glycaemic and cardiovascular risk. The beverage is vegetarian and gluten-free but contains dairy. A standard serving is approximately 350 g (one medium cup), delivering around 300 kcal and 35 g of sugar per serving. The glycaemic index is estimated at 43 (low) based on analogous chocolate milk beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), with a glycaemic load of approximately 17 per serving.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for 'chocolate latte cold' exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy ready-to-drink mocha coffee beverage data (FDC ID 171891) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central chocolate milk beverage entries. The input source record values (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with the adapted USDA profile and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney chocolate milk beverage entries (Atkinson et al. 2008). Medium confidence is appropriate given the absence of an exact approved-source match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this chocolate milk-coffee beverage category is not reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171891) as of 2026-04-17. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the primary adapted USDA source for this beverage category. 3. GI of 43 is an analogous estimate based on chocolate milk beverage entries (GI 34–43) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for chocolate latte cold exists. gi_category is 'low' (GI ≤ 55). 4. The input record listed duplicate portions (two identical entries for '1 standard cup, 350 g'); deduplicated to a single default portion of 350 g. 5. The input ethnic_tags included 'Cake' which is not applicable to this beverage; this tag has been removed. 6. The input country_of_origin is listed as Singapore; primary_cuisine has been updated to 'Singaporean / Southeast Asian café' to reflect the broader regional availability of this beverage style. 7. All core macros (protein_g 2.29, fat_g 3.43, carbohydrate_g 11.43) are non-zero and do not require zero-macro justification. 8. Nutrient source corrected to cite USDA FoodData Central exclusively as the approved primary source; the previous iteration incorrectly referenced a combined USDA/HPB citation in the nutrient_source title field. HPB Singapore Foodo was consulted but does not contain a directly matching entry and is noted only in source_match_notes. 9. GI category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to match GI value of 43 (low GI threshold: ≤ 55); glycemic_load_per_serving recalculated as (43 × 40.0) / 100 = 17.2, recorded as 17.1.
